intent: Prevent regression of B1 nested-if-continue copy-paste pattern (17 files / 19 sites). Single point-fix should auto-trigger population sweep; lint enforces it.

axes.interpretation: post-sweep clean — no remaining instances of the bad pattern detected by this lint

iteration_history.cause: outer-if of file_exists() flagged when inner while had its own flat-guard
loop-tail anchored on while/for, but `i = i + 2` and `i = nxt` not recognized as safe inner update
i = j + 1 reassign accepted but continue inside nested while still attributed to outer loop
skip continues inside nested while/for blocks

false_negatives_acknowledged: single-line nested forms `if c { if d { continue } }` not detected (rare in codebase)
non-`i` counter names (e.g. `idx`, `pos`) — lint is hardcoded to `i = i + 1` tail
loops with multi-statement tails or counters incremented at top of body
continue inside switch-like or match-like nested blocks if hexa adds them
axes.scope_intentional_limit: Lint targets the specific B1 idiom (`while i < n`-style index loop with `i = i + 1` tail). Broader detector would require AST/full hexa parser.

precommit_hook.reason: no .githooks/ directory and no active .git/hooks/pre-commit (only stock samples)
precommit_hook.next_cycle_action: after one bake cycle, create .githooks/pre-commit calling `tool/lint_nested_if_continue.sh` filtered to staged .hexa files; user opts in via `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`
precommit_hook.rationale: spec requires opt-in only; do not silently install hooks

method_gap_addressed: Yesterday a single point-fix was applied without the implicit population-sweep step. This lint encodes the population invariant: any future re-introduction of the pattern fails the lint; CI/hook adoption (next cycle) makes the sweep automatic instead of memory-dependent.
